mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and elevated recession probability, which reduces expected corporate earnings and drives negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered observations, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a walk-forward t-test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength and residential investment growth, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates robust manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in over 55% of observations during recovery phases, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark with 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 305 reflect entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing borrowing costs and compressing equity multiples over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal shows no significant out-of-sample predictive accuracy (AUC < 0.52).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate demonstrate accelerating labor market tightness, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in over 50% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Strong gross domestic product growth reflects robust aggregate demand and economic expansion, which translates directly into higher corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations, or the GDP signal exhibits a Rank IC below 0.03 across all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and consumer prices signal policy lag and persistent inflation, which severely compresses equity valuations and produces negative returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint monetary signal fails to predict negative returns with greater than 55% accuracy, or the interaction term loses economic significance during tightening periods.
